# Architecture

How AgentCenter fits together today. For *what we plan to build next*, see [`plan.md`](./plan.md). For deployment, see [`deploy.md`](./deploy.md).

## Topology

```text
Browser ──── Vercel (Next.js 16) ──── Neon (Postgres)
                ├── App Router pages (RSC + client islands)
                ├── /api/auth/...        Better Auth cookie sessions
                ├── /api/v1/...          Public registry API (CLI)
                ├── /api/upload/sign     R2 presigned PUT
                └── /api/inngest         Inngest webhook
                                              │
Browser ─── direct PUT ──→  Cloudflare R2 ←──┘
                                              │
                                          Inngest ──→ Neon + R2 (background jobs)

CLI (Bun-built binary) ──── /api/v1/... (Bearer token via device-code flow)
```

Web requests hit Vercel; the CLI hits the same Vercel deployment but talks only to the `/api/v1` surface. Bundles flow directly between the browser/CLI and R2 via signed URLs — Vercel never proxies binary content.

## Web app layout

The App Router tree is locale-segmented. URLs are always prefixed (`/en/...`, `/zh/...`); there is no implicit default.

```text
app/
├── layout.tsx                 # html/body, font loaders, theme cookie
├── [locale]/
│   ├── layout.tsx             # next-intl provider, AppShell (sidebar + topbar)
│   ├── page.tsx               # home (featured + trending)
│   ├── extensions/
│   │   ├── page.tsx           # browse (filter bar + grid)
│   │   └── [slug]/page.tsx    # detail (README + metadata panel)
│   ├── publish/
│   │   ├── page.tsx           # dashboard (my extensions)
│   │   └── new/page.tsx       # upload wizard
│   ├── (auth)/sign-in,sign-up
│   └── cli/auth/page.tsx      # device-code authorization UI
└── api/                       # see "API surfaces" below
```

### RSC vs client islands

Server components render the static shell, fetch data via Drizzle, and stream HTML. Client components are reserved for interactive bits — filter chips, the install button, the upload wizard, the user menu — and are marked with `"use client"`. The pattern is "server frame, client islands."

The filter bar (`components/filters/filter-bar.tsx`) is a server component but composes client islands (`FilterChips`, `SortSelect`, `DeptPicker`, `TagDrawer`). Each island reads/writes URL search params via `useFilterUrl`, so filter state lives in the URL — no global store, no context, and back/forward works.

### i18n

Locales live under `lib/i18n/messages/{en,zh}.json`. `next-intl` is configured with `localePrefix: "always"`. Static UI strings come from `useTranslations(...)`; bilingual content (extension `name` / `nameZh`, tag labels) is column-per-language in Postgres and resolved at the component level via `tagLabel(...)` / direct field access.

### Theme

`Editorial Ivory` (default) and `Dark`. The active theme is stored in a cookie (`theme`), read in the root layout, and applied as a `data-theme` attribute. The toggle (`ThemeSwitch`) writes the cookie and triggers `router.refresh()`.

## Request flows

### Browse

```text
GET /en/extensions?category=skills&dept=eng.cloud&sort=stars
  └─ app/[locale]/extensions/page.tsx (RSC)
      ├─ parseFilters(searchParams)
      ├─ Promise.all([listExtensions, countFilteredExtensions, getTagsWithCounts])
      └─ <FilterBar tags={tags} /> + <ExtGrid items={items} />
```

`buildExtensionWhere` / `buildExtensionOrder` in `lib/search/query.ts` compose Drizzle SQL clauses from filters; they're pure (no DB call), unit-tested, and shared with `/api/v1/extensions` so web and CLI see the same listing semantics.

### Detail

```text
GET /en/extensions/my-skill
  └─ app/[locale]/extensions/[slug]/page.tsx (RSC)
      ├─ getExtensionBySlug(slug)
      ├─ react-markdown + rehype-sanitize on the stored README
      └─ <ExtHero /> + <Markdown /> + <ExtMetadataPanel /> + <InstallButton />
```

The README is raw markdown stored in Postgres and server-rendered with `rehype-sanitize`. The metadata side panel (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, taxonomy) is a server component fed from the same row.

### Publish

```text
Browser                          Vercel                          R2          Inngest
   │ form submit                    │                              │             │
   ├──── createDraftExtension ────► server action  ──► extensions row (status=draft)
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── upload bundle ─────────────│─── presigned PUT URL ───────►│             │
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── attachFile(versionId) ────► server action  ──► files row + extensionVersion
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── submitForReview ──────────► server action  ──► sendEvent("extension/scan.requested")
   │                                │                              │             │
   │                                │                              │  scan-bundle:
   │                                │                              │   download from R2,
   │                                │                              │   sha-256 checksum,
   │                                │                              │   parse manifest.toml,
   │                                │                              │   validate schema,
   │                                │                              │   mark version ready/rejected,
   │                                │                              │   sendEvent("extension/index.requested")
   │                                │                              │             │
   │                                │                              │  reindex-search:
   │                                │                              │   build search_vector,
   │                                │                              │   set extension visibility=published,
   │                                │                              │   revalidateTag("extensions")
```

The upload goes browser → R2 directly (presigned PUT) — Vercel only signs the URL. The Inngest webhook lives at `/api/inngest`. Job source: `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` and `lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts`.

A note on `scan-bundle`: download + checksum + manifest parsing live in a single `step.run` rather than separate steps, because Inngest serializes step return values across boundaries and `Buffer` doesn't survive that round-trip.

### Install (CLI path)

```text
agentcenter install my-skill
  ├─ GET /api/v1/extensions/my-skill                     metadata (name, version, category)
  ├─ GET /api/v1/extensions/my-skill/bundle              302 → signed R2 URL
  ├─ fetch(signed-url)                                   ZIP bytes
  ├─ unzip + write to ~/.claude/skills/my-skill/         (or configured agent path)
  └─ POST /api/v1/installs (Bearer token)                records install event, increments downloadsCount
```

The web "Install" button takes the same conceptual path but goes through the `installExtension` server action instead of the public API: it bumps the same counters and records the same install event, so leaderboards stay consistent across surfaces.

## Authentication

Two parallel flows on top of one Better Auth instance:

- **Web** — cookie sessions. `signIn.email()` from `authClient` (`better-auth/react`) sets the cookie; subsequent server components read the session via `getSession()`. Client components subscribe via `authClient.useSession()` for reactive updates.
- **CLI** — device-code flow on top of Better Auth's `verifications` table.
  1. CLI: `POST /api/v1/auth/device/code` → server creates two `verifications` rows (`dc:poll:<deviceCode>` and `dc:user:<userCode>`) and returns the user code.
  2. User: opens `/{locale}/cli/auth` in a browser, signs in, enters the user code; the page resolves user code → device code, marks the poll row authorized, and stores a session token in it.
  3. CLI: polls `POST /api/v1/auth/device/poll` every 5 s. When the row is authorized, the token is returned once and the row is deleted.
  4. CLI persists the token in `~/.config/agentcenter/credentials.json` (mode 0600) and uses it as a `Bearer` for authenticated v1 calls.

The token is a Better Auth session token — the same kind a browser cookie carries — so the same `sessions` table backs both flows.

## API surfaces

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/api/auth/[...all]` | Better Auth — session cookies, sign-in, sign-up, sign-out (web) |
| `/api/v1/...` | Public registry API consumed by the CLI ([`api.md`](./api.md)) |
| `/api/upload/sign` | Web-internal — issues an R2 presigned PUT URL for the upload wizard |
| `/api/inngest` | Webhook — Inngest dispatches `scan-bundle` and `reindex-search` here |

Only `/api/v1/*` is treated as a contract; the others are implementation details.

## Data flow at a glance

```text
publish wizard ──→ extensions (draft) ──→ files + extensionVersions ──→ Inngest scan
                                                                           │
                          extensions (published, search_vector) ←──── reindex-search
                                            │
                          listExtensions (browse) ── installExtension / install API
                                                            │
                                                       installs row
                                                       collections "installed"
                                                       downloadsCount++
```

Every write that affects what users see in the listing (publish, install) ends with a `revalidateTag` so the next browse render hits fresh data.

+Both surfaces are thin wrappers around `recordInstall` in `lib/installs/record.ts`, which owns: extension lookup (by `id` from the web button or `slug` from the CLI), version resolution (an omitted version means "latest published" — `extension_versions` ordered by `publishedAt DESC` among `status='ready'`), the `installs` row insert, the `installed` collection upsert, and the atomic `downloadsCount` bump — all three writes go through `db.transaction`. Each call records a row, so `downloadsCount` is total install events; the `installed` collection is a separate, idempotent membership concept used by the UI's "Installed" view. Failures throw a typed `InstallError` (`extension_not_found`, `no_published_version`); wrappers translate to their surface (action union or HTTP status).

content.text:
Read both files. The version lifecycle is bigger than I initially scoped — there are **seven distinct DB transitions** across three files, not just the two scan-bundle blocks I flagged earlier. Let me lay them out before grilling.

### Current transitions

| # | Where | Writes | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `publish.ts:createDraftExtension` | `extensions(visibility=draft)` + `extensionVersions(status=pending)` + `extensionTags` | publish form submit |
| 2 | `publish.ts:attachFile` | `files(scanStatus=pending)` + `extensionVersions.bundleFileId` | bundle uploaded |
| 3 | `publish.ts:submitForReview` | `extensionVersions(status=scanning)` + Inngest event | "submit" click |
| 4 | `scan-bundle.ts` mark-rejected | `files(scanStatus=flagged, scanReport)` + `extensionVersions(status=rejected)` | scan failed |
| 5 | `scan-bundle.ts` mark-clean | `files(scanStatus=clean, scanReport, checksumSha256)` + `extensionVersions(status=ready)` | scan ok |
| 6 | `reindex-search.ts` update-search-vector | raw SQL: `extensions.search_vector` + `visibility=published` + `published_at` | index event |
| 7 | `reindex-search.ts` stamp-version | `extensionVersions.publishedAt` | index event |

Schema knowledge of `extensions`, `extensionVersions`, and `files` is spread across three files. The invariants are real ("a file is `clean` iff its version is `ready`"; "publish flips visibility *and* version.publishedAt — both must succeed") but enforced only by convention.

---

### Q1. What's the scope of the new module?

This is the biggest decision; everything else hangs off it.

- **(a) Everything** — `lib/extensions/state.ts` exposes `createDraft`, `attachBundle`, `submit`, `recordScanResult`, `publishVersion`. All seven transitions delegate. Both `publish.ts` and the jobs become thin.
- **(b) Just transitions, not creates** — module owns `submit`, `recordScanResult`, `publishVersion` (transitions of `status`, `scanStatus`, `visibility`). `createDraftExtension` and `attachFile` stay in `publish.ts` because they're transactionally coherent with form data and not duplicated.
- **(c) Only the jobs** — `recordScanResult`, `publishVersion`. The action-side stays put. Smallest scope; matches the original sketch.

My read: **(b) is the right line.** Creates *aren't* leaky — they stay close to the form they came from, and they're the only callers. The leak is *transitions* of status columns: those columns get touched from publish.ts (#3), scan-bundle.ts (#4, #5), and reindex-search.ts (#6, #7), and the invariants between them aren't centralized. Pulling creates into the module would just add a layer; pulling transitions concentrates the invariants.

(c) is defensible if you want the smallest possible change. (a) is over-broad — it bundles "form intake" and "lifecycle" into one module that has too many shapes of caller.

### Q2. How are scan results modeled?

The job today branches `if (!scanResult.ok)` and calls `mark-rejected` or `mark-clean`. Two ways to lift this:

- **(a) Two functions**: `markVersionRejected(versionId, fileId, reason)` and `markVersionReady(versionId, fileId, checksum, scanReport)`. Caller branches. Today's shape, just relocated.
- **(b) One function**: `recordScanResult(versionId, fileId, result)` where `result` is `{ ok: true, checksum, scanReport } | { ok: false, reason, scanReport }`. Module owns the union → SQL mapping.

(b) is the deeper interface. The "scan result" is a real concept; teaching every caller how to map success/failure onto two different write shapes is exactly what depth eliminates. The job becomes one line: `await recordScanResult(versionId, fileId, scanResult)`.

### Q3. Does `publishVersion` own revalidation and the published event?

Today the publish step in reindex-search.ts does four things: (1) DB writes, (2) DB writes, (3) `revalidateTag("extensions")`, (4) `inngest.sendEvent("extension/published")`.

- **(a)** Module = (1) + (2) only. Job orchestrates (3) and (4).
- **(b)** Module = (1) + (2) + (3). Job does (4) only.
- **(c)** Module = all four.

I'd lock **(a)**. Two reasons:
- `revalidateTag` and `sendEvent` are integration calls (Next.js cache, Inngest). Pulling them into the module ties it to those runtimes and makes it harder to test.
- The module's interface should be "version-state transitions." Cache invalidation and event publication are *consequences* of those transitions; the job composes them.

### Q4. Search vector — inside `publishVersion` or separate?

The raw `to_tsvector(...)` SQL is content-derived (reads `name`, `name_zh`, `description`, etc.). Today it only runs at publish-time. Two takes:

- **(a)** Keep it inside `publishVersion` — "this version becoming public" includes its content becoming searchable. One transition.
- **(b)** Split into `publishVersion` (visibility + timestamps) and `reindexExtension(extensionId)` (search_vector). Reindex would run on any content change, not just publish.

(a) matches today's behavior. (b) is correct *if* extensions get edited after publish — but I don't see an edit-after-publish path in the current code. Until that exists, (a) keeps locality high. Mark (b) as a future split when content-edit lands.

### Q5. The fallback path (`try/catch` around `to_tsvector`)

`reindex-search.ts:30-51` has a try/catch: try `UPDATE ... search_vector = to_tsvector(...) ...`, fall back to a plain visibility update if that fails. The comment says "added in the P12 FTS migration; until then the raw SQL falls back gracefully."

Quick question: is P12 done? If yes, the fallback is dead code we can delete. If no, we keep it. (Easy lookup — I can grep for the migration. I'll check before writing.) Not blocking the design.

### Q6. Module location

I'd default to `lib/extensions/state.ts`, sibling to `lib/installs/record.ts`. Future siblings (`lib/extensions/manifest.ts` for candidate #3, `lib/extensions/queries.ts` if we ever lift the data layer) fit the same namespace. Push back if you'd rather it be `lib/jobs/lifecycle.ts` or somewhere else.

---

**Pick (a/b/c) for Q1, Q2, Q3. Confirm or reject Q4, Q5, Q6.** Q1 is the load-bearing one — the rest are easier once that's locked.

content.text:
Important finding before continuing. The migration `0002_fts_search_vector.sql:8-16` defines `extensions.search_vector` as:

```sql
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "search_vector" tsvector
  GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED;
```

It's a **generated column** — Postgres computes it from `name`, `name_zh`, `tagline`, `description`, `description_zh` automatically on every row update. The schema file confirms this (lib/db/schema/extension.ts:22-25 comment: "GENERATED ALWAYS (read-only); never written by app code").

This makes `reindex-search.ts:30-52` mostly dead code:

- The `try` block does `UPDATE extensions SET search_vector = to_tsvector('english', ...)`. Postgres rejects writes to a generated column → the catch swallows the error.
- The "fallback" (`UPDATE extensions SET visibility='published', publishedAt=now()`) is what *actually* runs every time.
- The raw SQL also uses `to_tsvector('english', ...)` while the generated column uses `'simple'` with weighted `setweight(..., 'A'/'B'/'C')`. Even if it weren't generated, the manual update would degrade ranking quality.

So Q4 partially dissolves — there's no search vector for `publishVersion` to maintain. Postgres handles it. The whole `update-search-vector` step collapses to "flip visibility + published_at."

### Revised plan

**`lib/extensions/state.ts`** owns three transitions:

```ts
export async function submit(versionId: string): Promise<void>
  // version.status: pending → scanning

export async function recordScanResult(
  versionId: string,
  fileId: string,
  result:
    | { ok: true; checksum: string; scanReport: unknown }
    | { ok: false; reason: string; scanReport: unknown },
): Promise<void>
  // ok=true:  files(scanStatus=clean, scanReport, checksumSha256) + version(status=ready)
  // ok=false: files(scanStatus=flagged, scanReport)               + version(status=rejected)
  // tx in both branches

export async function publishVersion(versionId: string): Promise<{ extensionId: string }>
  // tx: extensions(visibility=published, publishedAt=now()) + version(publishedAt=now())
  // returns extensionId so the job can revalidate / sendEvent
```

**Callers shrink to:**

- `lib/actions/publish.ts:submitForReview` calls `submit(versionId)` instead of inline update + still sends the Inngest event itself (event-sending is orchestration).
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` calls `recordScanResult(versionId, fileId, scanResult)` once. Both `mark-rejected` and `mark-clean` step blocks collapse.
- `lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts` calls `publishVersion(versionId)`, then does `revalidateTag("extensions")` and `sendEvent("extension/published")`. The dead try/catch + raw SQL is deleted.

**Tests** (mock `@/lib/db/client` per project convention):
- `submit` flips status correctly
- `recordScanResult` ok-branch issues both writes
- `recordScanResult` failure-branch issues both writes
- `publishVersion` returns extensionId, issues both writes

### One open question

`publishVersion` updates `extensions.publishedAt` to `now()` *every time* a version of that extension publishes. Today that means: if a publisher releases v1.1.0 a year after v1.0.0, the `extensions.publishedAt` column resets to "now." Is that intentional? Two options:

- **(a)** Keep current behavior. `publishedAt` = "most recent publish event." Used in browse sort = "what's been re-published recently."
- **(b)** Set only on first publish. `COALESCE(publishedAt, now())`. `publishedAt` = "when this extension first became public."

Looking at `lib/db/queries/extensions.ts` would settle which definition the listing actually uses. Quick lookup needed. Not blocking — I can preserve current behavior (a) and flag (b) as a separate question.

I'll lock (a) unless you push back.

---

**Sign off on the revised plan?** If yes, I'll create tasks and start implementing. The dead-code deletion in `reindex-search.ts` is a small bonus — flagging it as part of the same commit since it's load-bearing context for why `publishVersion` is so simple.

The upload goes browser → R2 directly (presigned PUT) — Vercel only signs the URL. The Inngest webhook lives at `/api/inngest`. Job source: `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` and `lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts`.

A note on `scan-bundle`: download + checksum + manifest parsing live in a single `step.run` rather than separate steps, because Inngest serializes step return values across boundaries and `Buffer` doesn't survive that round-trip.

### Version state transitions

`lib/extensions/state.ts` owns every transition of `extensionVersions.status`, `files.scanStatus`, and `extensions.visibility`. Three functions:

- `submit(versionId)` — moves a version from `pending` to `scanning` (called by `submitForReview` before it dispatches the scan event).
- `recordScanResult(versionId, fileId, result)` — applies the outcome of a bundle scan in one transaction; success sets `files.scanStatus='clean' + version.status='ready' + checksumSha256`, failure sets `'flagged' + 'rejected'`. The job calls this once with a discriminated `result`.
- `publishVersion(versionId)` — flips `extensions.visibility='published'` and stamps both rows with `publishedAt`, returning the `extensionId` for the job's downstream `revalidateTag` and `extension/published` event.

`extensions.search_vector` is a Postgres `GENERATED ALWAYS ... STORED` column (see `drizzle/0002_fts_search_vector.sql`); no application code writes it. The `reindex-search` job's earlier hand-rolled `to_tsvector` UPDATE was dead — the generated column always reflects the source content.

Committed `bc95892` on the same branch as the install refactor.

```
6 files changed, 267 insertions(+), 70 deletions(-)
 create lib/extensions/state.ts
 create lib/extensions/state.test.ts
 modified lib/actions/publish.ts
 modified lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts
 modified lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts
 modified docs/architecture.md
```

All 139 tests pass, lint + typecheck clean.

## Net result

| File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` | two `step.run` blocks each opening their own tx, hand-coding two columns × two tables | one `record-scan-result` step that delegates |
| `lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts` | 73 lines incl. dead `to_tsvector` UPDATE in a try/catch | 36 lines: publish + revalidate + event |
| `lib/actions/publish.ts:submitForReview` | inline status update | `await submit(versionId)` |
| `lib/extensions/state.ts` | — | new home for `submit`, `recordScanResult`, `publishVersion` |

## A bonus the dig surfaced

The hand-rolled `to_tsvector` UPDATE in the old `reindex-search.ts` always threw — `extensions.search_vector` has been a `GENERATED ALWAYS ... STORED` column since the P12 migration. The `try/catch` swallowed the exception every time and the "fallback" plain visibility update was the only path that ran. Postgres has been maintaining the search vector from content all along; the application code was never participating. Deleted the dead branch in this commit and noted it in `docs/architecture.md`.

## Branch state

Both refactors (install recording + version lifecycle) are on `refactor/install-recording`. If you want them as two separate PRs, easiest split:

```
git checkout main
git checkout -b refactor/version-lifecycle
git cherry-pick bc95892
git checkout refactor/install-recording
git reset --hard 9974bd0   # drops the lifecycle commit
```

Or push the branch as-is and split at PR time. Your call. Want to keep going (manifest schema is next), or stop here?

input.command:
gh pr create --title "refactor: deepen install recording and version lifecycle modules" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Two related refactors, both surfacing modules that were SHALLOW (interface nearly as complex as implementation, schema knowledge spread across callers) and turning them DEEP (a lot of behaviour behind a small interface). Same theme: concentrate invariants in one place, shrink callers to thin wrappers.

### 1. Install recording (`lib/installs/record.ts`)

`lib/actions/install.ts` (web button) and `app/api/v1/installs/route.ts` (CLI) were running near-identical 4-step recipes: lookup ext → get system collection → check membership → insert + upsert + bump counter. The two had already drifted — the CLI did a non-atomic read-then-write on `downloadsCount`. New module owns lookup (by `id` or `slug`), version resolution (omitted = latest published), and three writes inside one `db.transaction`. Returns `{ installId, isFirstInstall, version }`; throws typed `InstallError`.

Behavioural changes:
- Every install records a row and bumps the counter (no dedupe via the collection). `downloadsCount` now means total install events; the `installed` collection is a separate idempotent membership concept.
- The web action no longer writes the literal string `"latest"` into `installs.version`. The API route translates the legacy `"latest"` sentinel from CLI clients to `undefined` for backwards compatibility.
- Atomic counter increment everywhere — was non-atomic in the CLI route.

### 2. Version lifecycle (`lib/extensions/state.ts`)

Seven distinct DB transitions touching `extensionVersions.status`, `files.scanStatus`, and `extensions.visibility` were spread across `publish.ts`, `scan-bundle.ts`, and `reindex-search.ts`. Three transitions extracted into one module (creates stay where they are):

- `submit(versionId)` — pending → scanning
- `recordScanResult(versionId, fileId, result)` — single discriminated-union call replaces the two `mark-rejected` / `mark-clean` step blocks; each branch is one transaction
- `publishVersion(versionId)` — flips visibility + stamps `publishedAt` on both rows, returns `extensionId` for the caller's revalidate / sendEvent

### 3. Dead-code deletion in `reindex-search.ts`

The hand-rolled `UPDATE extensions SET search_vector = to_tsvector(...)` always threw — `extensions.search_vector` has been a Postgres `GENERATED ALWAYS ... STORED` column since the P12 migration (`drizzle/0002_fts_search_vector.sql`). The `try/catch` swallowed the exception every time and the "fallback" plain visibility update was the only path that ran. Postgres maintains the column from source content automatically; the application code was never participating. Deleted the dead branch and documented the contract in `docs/architecture.md`.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run lint` — clean
- [x] `bun run typecheck` — clean
- [x] `bun run test` — 139 tests pass (was 134; 11 new tests across `lib/installs/record.test.ts` and `lib/extensions/state.test.ts`, mocking `@/lib/db/client` per project convention)
- [ ] CI runs on this PR
- [ ] Smoke-check the web Install button on a published extension — should show "Installed ✓" after click, button stays disabled for 3 s
- [ ] Smoke-check the CLI install path against a deployed branch — should record a row with the actual semver, not `"latest"`
- [ ] Verify `downloadsCount` increments by exactly 1 per click (atomic SQL increment)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

CodeRabbit posted 5 actionable comments + 10 nitpicks. Triage:

**Definitely fixing:**
1. **docs/api.md** — didn't update for new response shape (`{ ok, alreadyInstalled }` → `{ ok, installId, isFirstInstall, version }`). My miss.
2. **`submit()` lacks state guard** — should enforce `pending → scanning`. Without it, Inngest retries or stale UI can shove a version backward through the state machine.
3. **`recordScanResult()` lacks state guard** — same. Inngest at-least-once means a duplicate delivery could overwrite a terminal state. This is a real correctness bug given the runtime.
4. **`publishVersion()` lacks ready check** — same family.
5. **`isFirstInstall` race** — the prior-check is outside the transaction. Two concurrent installs both see "no prior" and both report `isFirstInstall: true`. Move inside the tx.

**Quick wins also fixing:**
6. **Latest-version tiebreaker** — `ORDER BY publishedAt DESC` alone is non-deterministic on ties.
7. **500 fallback in install route** — `throw e` for non-`InstallError` exits the consistent `jsonError` envelope. Catch-all → 500 with the right shape.
8. **state.test.ts where-predicate assertions** — current tests would pass if a bug scoped updates to the wrong rows.

**Skipping with reasons:**
- "Drop `latest` sentinel" — contract change, low payoff.
- "Avoid non-null assertion" — cosmetic.
- "Validate explicit version exists" — bearer auth gates this, not a security concern; recording a bogus version is harmless garbage.

Note on the state-machine guards (#2-#4): these turn out to be the deeper version of the original refactor's intent. The "version lifecycle" module isn't actually a state machine until the preconditions are enforced — without them it's still callers all the way down.

# Public registry API

The `/api/v1/*` surface is the contract between the web app and the CLI (or any third-party tool that wants to talk to AgentCenter). Other API routes (`/api/auth`, `/api/upload/sign`, `/api/inngest`) are implementation details and are not documented here.

All paths are relative to the registry base URL — `https://agentcenter.app` in production, or whatever `registry` value is set in the CLI's `~/.config/agentcenter/config.toml`.

## Conventions

- Content type is JSON unless otherwise noted.
- All write endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. The token is obtained via the device-code flow ([`POST /auth/device/code`](#post-apiv1authdevicecode)).
- Errors return JSON with the shape `{ "error": "<code>", "message": "<human-readable>" }` and a 4xx/5xx status. Common codes: `unauthenticated` (401), `not_found` (404), `invalid_body` (400), `bundle_unavailable` (503), `server_error` (500).
- Listing endpoints set `Cache-Control: public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300`.

## Authentication — device code flow

The CLI is headless. It exchanges a short-lived **device code** (server-side, opaque) for a **user code** (short, human-readable) the user types into the browser. Once the user authorizes, the CLI polls for a session token.

### `POST /api/v1/auth/device/code`

Start a login. No auth required.

**Response**

```json
{
  "deviceCode": "f9b6c63a-…",
  "userCode": "ABCD-1234",
  "verificationUri": "/cli/auth",
  "expiresIn": 600
}
```

The `verificationUri` is a path on the registry; the CLI is expected to combine it with the registry base URL and open it in the user's browser.

### `POST /api/v1/auth/device/poll`

Poll for authorization. No auth required. The CLI polls every 5 s.

**Request**

```json
{ "deviceCode": "f9b6c63a-…" }
```

**Response — pending**

```json
{ "status": "pending" }
```

**Response — authorized** (returned exactly once; the row is then deleted)

```json
{ "status": "authorized", "token": "<session-token>" }
```

**Response — expired or unknown**

```json
{ "status": "expired" }
```

The `token` is a Better Auth session token. The CLI stores it in `~/.config/agentcenter/credentials.json` (mode 0600) and sends it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on subsequent calls.

## Extensions

### `GET /api/v1/extensions`

List published extensions. No auth required.

**Query parameters** (all optional)

| Param | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `q` | string | Free-text search (Postgres FTS + `pg_trgm`) |
| `category` | `skills` \| `mcp` \| `slash` \| `plugins` | Filter by extension category |
| `scope` | `personal` \| `org` \| `enterprise` | Filter by scope |
| `funcCat` | `workTask` \| `business` \| `tools` | Filter by functional category |
| `subCat` | string | Filter by L1 subcategory |
| `tags` | comma-separated | e.g. `tags=search,api` |
| `tagMatch` | `any` \| `all` | Tag match mode (default `any`) |
| `filter` | `official` \| `trending` \| `new` | Curated filter |
| `sort` | `downloads` \| `stars` \| `recent` | Sort order (default `downloads`) |
| `page` | integer ≥ 1 | Page number, page size is fixed at 24 |

**Response**

```json
{
  "items": [
    {
      "slug": "web-search",
      "name": "Web Search",
      "nameZh": "网页搜索",
      "category": "skills",
      "scope": "personal",
      "badge": "official",
      "description": "Search the web from your agent.",
      "descriptionZh": "在你的智能体中搜索网页。",
      "tags": ["search", "api"],
      "funcCat": "workTask",
      "subCat": "softDev",
      "l2": "frontend",
      "downloadsCount": 1248,
      "starsAvg": "4.7"
    }
  ],
  "total": 87,
  "page": 1,
  "pageSize": 24
}
```

### `GET /api/v1/extensions/:slug`

Full metadata for a single extension. No auth required.

**Response**

```json
{
  "slug": "web-search",
  "name": "Web Search",
  "nameZh": "网页搜索",
  "category": "skills",
  "scope": "personal",
  "badge": "official",
  "tagline": "One-line tagline",
  "description": "Longer description.",
  "descriptionZh": "更长的描述。",
  "tags": ["search", "api"],
  "funcCat": "workTask",
  "subCat": "softDev",
  "l2": "frontend",
  "license": "MIT",
  "homepageUrl": "https://example.com",
  "repoUrl": "https://github.com/example/web-search",
  "compatibilityJson": { "agent": "claude", "minVersion": "1.0" },
  "downloadsCount": 1248,
  "starsAvg": "4.7",
  "ratingsCount": 42,
  "publishedAt": "2026-01-15T08:30:00.000Z",
  "version": "latest",
  "bundleUrl": "/api/v1/extensions/web-search/bundle"
}
```

`version` is currently the literal string `"latest"`; full version history is not yet exposed.

`bundleUrl` is a relative path to [`GET /api/v1/extensions/:slug/bundle`](#get-apiv1extensionsslugbundle) — clients should resolve it against the registry base URL.

**Errors**

- `404 not_found` — slug doesn't exist or isn't published.

### `GET /api/v1/extensions/:slug/bundle`

Download the latest ready bundle. No auth required. Returns a **302 redirect** to a Cloudflare R2 signed URL — clients should follow it (or read the `Location` header if they want to log the URL).

**Errors**

- `404 not_found` — slug doesn't exist.
- `503 bundle_unavailable` — extension exists but no version has reached `ready` status (still scanning, or scan failed).

The signed URL is valid for ~5 minutes. Clients should fetch the bundle immediately rather than caching the redirect.

## Installs

### `POST /api/v1/installs`

Record a successful install. **Requires** `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

The CLI calls this *after* it has unzipped the bundle and written files locally — so even if the registry is unreachable at install time, the local install still succeeds.

**Request**

```json
{
  "extensionSlug": "web-search",
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "agentName": "claude",
  "agentVersion": "1.0.4"
}
```

`agentName` and `agentVersion` are informational — stored for analytics but not validated against an agent registry.

**Response**

```json
{ "ok": true, "alreadyInstalled": false }
```

If the user has already installed this extension on a previous run, `alreadyInstalled: true` and no counters are bumped.

**Errors**

- `401 unauthenticated` — missing or invalid Bearer token.
- `404 not_found` — slug doesn't exist.
- `400 invalid_body` — body failed schema validation.

## Versioning

The surface above is `/api/v1`. Breaking changes will introduce `/api/v2/...` — the `v1` namespace will not change incompatibly. Additive changes (new optional fields, new endpoints) may land within `v1` without notice.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(installs,state): address coderabbit review on PR #4

Lifecycle state guards (the deeper version of the original refactor):
- submit: WHERE id=? AND status='pending' with .returning() check; throws
  VersionStateError when the row is missing or not pending.
- recordScanResult: pre-reads version status; throws if not 'scanning'.
  scan-bundle.ts catches VersionStateError and treats it as a no-op so
  duplicate Inngest deliveries don't poison terminal states.
- publishVersion: pre-reads status; throws unless 'ready'.

Without these guards the module wasn't really a state machine — late
deliveries or stale UI could shove versions backward through the graph.

Race-safer first-install signal:
- Move the prior-install lookup inside the recordInstall transaction so
  both reads see the same snapshot. Still best-effort under READ
  COMMITTED, but no longer a window between two unrelated queries.

Smaller cleanups:
- Latest-version lookup orders by publishedAt DESC, createdAt DESC for a
  deterministic tiebreaker on rapid publishes / backfills.
- /api/v1/installs catch-all returns jsonError(500, "internal_error")
  instead of rethrowing — keeps the error envelope consistent.
- docs/api.md updated for the new install response shape; CodeRabbit
  caught that the docs still showed { ok, alreadyInstalled }.
- Strengthened lifecycle tests: cover the new throw paths and assert
  that .where(...) is called for each update (catches wrong-row bugs).
EOF
)" && git push 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
Pushed `4b18949` to the PR.

## Addressed 5 actionable + 3 quick-win nitpicks

| Comment | Fix |
|---|---|
| `submit()` lacks state guard | `WHERE id=? AND status='pending'` + `.returning()` check, throws `VersionStateError` |
| `recordScanResult()` lacks state guard | Pre-reads status, throws if not `scanning`; scan-bundle catches and no-ops (idempotent Inngest retries) |
| `publishVersion()` lacks ready check | Pre-reads status; throws unless `ready` |
| `isFirstInstall` race | Prior-install lookup moved inside the tx |
| docs/api.md stale | Updated to new response shape |
| Non-deterministic latest | Added `desc(createdAt)` tiebreaker |
| Silent rethrow → 500 | Catch-all returns `jsonError(500, "internal_error")` |
| Tests don't assert `where` | Added `.where` call assertions to each lifecycle test |

## Skipped with reasons

- "Drop `latest` sentinel from request schema" — contract change, low payoff for v0.1.
- "Avoid non-null assertion" — addressed indirectly (the `version!` is gone because `resolvedVersion` is now declared after the resolution branch).
- "Validate explicit version exists for the extension" — bearer auth gates this; recording an unknown version is harmless garbage, not a security concern.

143 tests pass (was 139; 4 new tests cover the state-guard throw paths). The state-guard work is the deeper version of the original refactor — without those preconditions the module wasn't really a state machine. CodeRabbit's review surfaced that.

- **Silent rethrow on /api/v1/installs** — non-`InstallError` exceptions now return `jsonError(500, "internal_error")` and log via `console.error`, keeping the response envelope consistent.

Thanks for the review — the state-machine guards in particular were the deeper version of the original refactor's intent.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

import { z } from "zod";

export const ManifestFormSchema = z.object({
  slug: z
    .string()
    .min(3)
    .max(80)
    .regex(/^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/, "Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only"),
  name: z.string().min(2).max(80),
  nameZh: z.string().max(80).optional(),
  version: z
    .string()
    .regex(/^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/, "Must be semver (e.g. 1.0.0)"),
  category: z.enum(["skills", "mcp", "slash", "plugins"]),
  scope: z.enum(["personal", "org", "enterprise"]),
  funcCat: z.enum(["workTask", "business", "tools"]),
  subCat: z.string().min(1).max(60),
  l2: z.string().max(60).optional(),
  deptId: z.string().optional(),
  tagIds: z.array(z.string()).max(8),
  description: z.string().max(300).optional(),
  descriptionZh: z.string().max(300).optional(),
  tagline: z.string().max(120).optional(),
  homepageUrl: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal("")),
  repoUrl: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal("")),
  licenseSpdx: z.string().max(40).optional(),
});

export type ManifestFormValues = z.infer<typeof ManifestFormSchema>;

# AgentCenter Extension Manifest Spec

Every extension bundle (`.zip`) **must** contain a `manifest.toml` at the archive root.
The manifest declares identity, discoverability, compatibility, and per-agent install targets.

---

## Top-level sections

### `[extension]` — required

content.text:
Good news: there's a `docs/manifest-spec.md` that's the canonical source of truth (171 lines). Both existing schemas have *each* drifted from it. Concrete catalog:

| Field | Spec | Form (`manifest.ts`) | Bundle (`scan-bundle.ts`) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `slug` | regex + max 64 | regex + min 3 + max **80** | min 1 + max **64** + no regex |
| `name` | (no length) | min 2 + max 80 | **min 1** |
| `description` | max 280 | max **300**, optional | max 280, **required** |
| `version` | semver | semver | semver |
| `tagline` | (allowed) | max 120 | **missing** |
| `nameZh` / `descriptionZh` | allowed | optional | **missing** |

Three drifts at once: form↔spec, bundle↔spec, form↔bundle. Each accepts data the other rejects.

### Proposed design (one schema module)

`lib/validators/extension-manifest.ts` (replaces `manifest.ts`) exports three schemas built on shared primitives:

```ts
// Single source of truth — matches docs/manifest-spec.md exactly.
export const ExtensionManifestCore = z.object({
  slug:        z.string().min(3).max(64).regex(SLUG_RE, "..."),
  name:        z.string().min(2).max(80),
  nameZh:      z.string().max(80).optional(),
  version:     z.string().regex(SEMVER_RE, "..."),
  category:    z.enum(["skills", "mcp", "slash", "plugins"]),
  scope:       z.enum(["personal", "org", "enterprise"]),
  funcCat:     z.enum(["workTask", "business", "tools"]),
  subCat:      z.string().min(1).max(60),
  l2:          z.string().max(60).optional(),
  description: z.string().max(280).optional(),
  descriptionZh: z.string().max(280).optional(),
  tagline:     z.string().max(120).optional(),
});

// Form: core + UI-only fields.
export const FormManifestSchema = ExtensionManifestCore.extend({
  tagIds:      z.array(z.string()).max(8),
  deptId:      z.string().optional(),
  homepageUrl: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal("")),
  repoUrl:     z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal("")),
  licenseSpdx: z.string().max(40).optional(),
});

// Bundle: core lifted into TOML sections.
export const BundleManifestSchema = z.object({
  extension:     ExtensionManifestCore.pick({ slug, name, nameZh, version, category, scope, description, descriptionZh, tagline }),
  categorization: ExtensionManifestCore.pick({ funcCat, subCat, l2 }),
});
```

### Two decisions worth grilling

**Q1. Tighten the bundle?**

Today's bundle schema is more permissive than the form (no slug regex, `name.min(1)`, description required at max 280). Adopting `ExtensionManifestCore` *tightens* the bundle: a publisher whose `manifest.toml` had `name = "x"` (1 char) used to scan-pass and would now fail. That's the point — we're catching what the form has been catching all along. But if there are real published bundles in production already, this could reject them on re-scan.

- **(a)** Tighten — the canonical is the truth, bundles meet it.
- **(b)** Keep bundle permissive — relax `min(1)`, no slug regex, optional description. The bundle gets a *looser* derived schema that opts out of some constraints.

I'd pick (a). Catching weak data at scan-time is better than letting it propagate. v0.1 probably has zero bundles in the wild yet. Push back if you know otherwise.

**Q2. Description: optional or required?**

Form has `description.optional()`. Bundle has `description` required. Spec table marks it ✓ (required).

- **(a)** Required everywhere — match the spec. Form gets a small change; users must write a description to publish.
- **(b)** Optional everywhere — looser. Bundle relaxes.
- **(c)** Different in form vs bundle (today's drifted state, codified).

(a) is the spec's intent. Form change is real but minor — anyone publishing should describe their thing.

Other field divergences (slug max, description length) all resolve to "match the spec, no decision needed."

---

**Pick (a/b) for Q1 and (a/b/c) for Q2.** Sketching tasks now; will execute once you commit.

gh pr create --title "refactor(manifest): single source of truth for manifest validation" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

`lib/validators/manifest.ts` (form) and `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` (bundle) each defined their own zod schema for what counts as a valid extension manifest. Both had drifted from `docs/manifest-spec.md` and from each other:

| Field | Spec | Form (was) | Bundle (was) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `slug` | regex + max 64 | regex + max **80** | min 1 + max 64 + **no regex** |
| `name` | — | min 2 + max 80 | **min 1** |
| `description` | max 280, ✓ required | max **300**, **optional** | max 280, ✓ required |
| `tagline`, `nameZh`, `descriptionZh` | allowed | optional | **missing** |

Result: a bundle whose `manifest.toml` would have failed the publish form passed the scanner silently.

## Design

`lib/validators/manifest.ts` now exports `ExtensionManifestCore` — a single zod schema mirroring `docs/manifest-spec.md`. The two consumer-facing schemas derive from it:

- **`ManifestFormSchema`** = `core.extend({ tagIds, deptId, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx })` (UI-only fields the wizard collects).
- **`BundleManifestSchema`** = the same core fields lifted into `{ extension: ..., categorization: ... }` TOML sections via `.pick()`.

`scan-bundle.ts` imports the bundle schema instead of redefining it. The shared `SLUG_PATTERN` / `SEMVER_PATTERN` constants are exported so the form's hand-rolled `validate()` doesn't fork the regex.

## Behavioural changes

- **Bundle validation tightens to the spec** — slug regex, `name.min(2)`, `description.min(1)`. Bundles that were silently accepted before (e.g. 1-char names, missing description) are now rejected at scan time. v0.1 has no production bundles yet, so this is a forward-looking tightening.
- **Form description becomes required** to match the spec. `manifest-form.tsx` surfaces the inline error; the textarea `maxLength` changes 300 → 280.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run lint` — clean
- [x] `bun run typecheck` — clean
- [x] `bun run test` — 148 tests pass (143 → 148; 7 new tests on the shared core, bundle shape, and the new drift-fix invariants — slug max 64, description required, name min 2)
- [ ] CI runs on this PR
- [ ] Smoke-check the publish wizard: empty description should now block submission with an inline error, valid manifest should still publish end-to-end
- [ ] Smoke-check that an existing test bundle (if any) still scans cleanly under the tightened schema
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
